danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite manifestation in dungeons and overworld; demon designs by Kazuma Kaneko featuring mythological and otherworldly aesthetics
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat; world invasion by demonic forces ongoing

danger_signals.visual_form: Post-apocalyptic Tokyo environment after 30-year time skip; environmental decay and damage visible in overworld tileset
danger_signals.meaning: Catastrophic world state; demon invasion has devastated civilization

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual/mechanical indicator when magnetite fuel runs low; summoned demons take damage
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity danger; player vulnerability in hostile territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Status condition indicators on character/demon portraits during battle

safety_signals.visual_form: Special sanctuary location marked on world map; distinct environmental architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Neutral demon fusion zone; safe space for strategic preparation

safety_signals.visual_form: Scattered shops in accessible areas with NPC merchants; distinct visual markers on map
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition hub; safe trading zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Special terminal structures placed throughout Tokyo; distinct visual identifier
safety_signals.meaning: Safe checkpoint; progress preservation point; teleportation network node

safety_signals.visual_form: Order of Messiah churches and Ring of Gaia locations with faction-specific architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Faction-aligned player welcome; depending on protagonist alignment, area is accessible/safe or restricted

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Law-aligned demon designs associated with this faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Churches and religious sanctuaries throughout post-apocalyptic Tokyo; theological aesthetic in architecture

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Chaos-aligned demon designs associated with this faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cult gathering sites; nature-influenced or dark ritual spaces throughout Tokyo

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Computer program ability to communicate with demons; technologically mediated power
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Demon recruitment reflects protagonist alignment; visual demon party composition changes based on alignment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Access restrictions to faction territories based on protagonist alignment choice; some areas blocked to law/chaos players

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Near-future urban environment; functioning civilization; modern SNES-era cityscape graphics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stable NPC behavior; accessible civilian zones; normal trade and communication possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ruined buildings; destroyed infrastructure; overgrown/damaged terrain; demon-infested environments; darker color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostile demon encounters frequent; factions competing for control; protagonist transported forward; resource scarcity emphasized

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Territorial markers; faction-aligned NPC appearances; restricted area access based on alignment; environmental design reflects competing ideologies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs react differently to protagonist alignment; law-aligned demons refuse chaos players; faction locations become accessible/blocked based on choices

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High density of demon sprites in dungeons and overworld; demonic environmental corruption; rift visual effects in key locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Constant combat encounters; magnetite resource drain during exploration; demon negotiation/recruitment mechanics available

environmental_storytelling.example: Order of Messiah churches use religious iconography and organized architecture; Ring of Gaia locations feature occult/natural aesthetics; visual difference communicates ideological opposition without explicit text
Certain areas become visually inaccessible or restricted based on protagonist alignment; blocked doorways and faction guards communicate faction control
Visual degradation of Tokyo from functioning city to post-apocalyptic ruins after time-skip communicates catastrophic world-state change; no explicit exposition needed
Merchant locations, healing centers, and save terminals scattered strategically in world; their visual placement communicates safe zones within hostile territory
Kazuma Kaneko's mythological demon designs; law-aligned vs chaos-aligned vs neutral demons have distinct visual characteristics communicating their ideological nature

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Refuses alliance; displays rejection in dialogue; may become hostile
Refuses alliance; displays rejection in dialogue; alignment mismatch prevents recruitment
Can be asked for items/money, convinced to leave, or recruited; dialogue-based outcome varies by demon and protagonist alignment
Takes damage over time while walking in dungeons; visual/mechanical indicator of resource stress
Blocks access to faction territory; refuses aid; visual barrier communication
Refuses entry to churches; creates access restriction based on ideological opposition
Creates access restriction to Gaia sites; opposition visible in NPC interaction

notes: Shin Megami Tensei's visual communication relies heavily on alignment-based mechanics manifesting as environmental and behavioral restrictions. The game uses architectural faction identity (churches vs occult spaces), demon aesthetic theming (law/chaos/neutral visual categories), and access-gating as primary visual storytelling tools. The 30-year time-skip creates stark visual contrast between functioning and post-apocalyptic Tokyo. Magnetite depletion provides mechanical-visual feedback of resource vulnerability. The system emphasizes player choice consequences through visual faction territory control and NPC behavioral changes. Demon negotiation mechanic allows visual party composition to reflect alignment philosophy.
